title: sempervivum dolomiticum
intro: Sempervivum dolomiticum, also known as the dolomite houseleek, is a species of succulent plant that belongs to the genus Sempervivum, a group of rosette-forming plants commonly known as live forever or hen and chicks. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sempervivum dolomiticum include reducing inflammation, wound healing, and protecting against infections. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into infusions, salves, or ointments, which can be applied topically to treat various skin conditions and wounds.
